name: La Piedra Aguda Reservoir a la Encina Madre — circular desde Valverde de Leganés
region_intro: Mountain biking around Valverde De Leganés offers diverse landscapes, featuring extensive meadows, olive groves, and wooded pastures known as dehesas. The region is characterized by serene rural environments and varied terrain suitable for cycling. Natural features like the Piedra Aguda Reservoir provide technical single-track trails, while ecological corridors along the Alcarrache River contribute to the area's biodiversity. This combination of open spaces and specific trail features makes it an appealing destination for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Valverde De Leganés The most popular mountain bike trail is Dehesas de Olivenza — circular por el entorno del embalse del lago Alqueva (ruta 2), a 35.1 miles (56.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 37 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route navigates through dehesas near the Alqueva reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Valverde de Leganés, Olivenza, and San Jorge de Alor Loop, a moderate 29.5 miles (47.4 km) path. This route connects several local towns, offering varied terrain for an engaging ride. Local mountain bikers also love the Sierra de Alor Loop from Olivenza, a 27.7 miles (44.6 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Alor area, often completed in about 4 hours 1 minute. This path explores a region known for its panoramic views. Mountain biking around Valverde De Leganés is defined by dehesas, olive groves, and the Piedra Aguda Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Valverde De Leganés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 30 reviews. Nearly 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Valverde De Leganés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Valverde De Leganés?
A: There are nearly 60 mountain bike trails around Valverde De Leganés, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy, scenic rides to more challenging routes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Valverde De Leganés?
A: Yes, Valverde De Leganés offers 9 easy mountain bike trails, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes often feature gentler terrain through the picturesque dehesas and olive groves.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Valverde De Leganés?
A: The terrain around Valverde De Leganés is diverse, featuring extensive meadows, olive groves, and wooded pastures known as dehesas. You'll also find technical single-track trails, particularly along the edge of the Piedra Aguda Reservoir, and historical routes like the Cañada Real Sancha Brava.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Valverde De Leganés?
A: The spring months, from March to May, are highly recommended for mountain biking in Valverde De Leganés. The weather is mild, and the natural scenery is vibrant with blooming flowers, making for a very pleasant riding experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027139407/dehesas-de-olivenza-circular-por-el-entorno-del-embalse-del-lago-alqueva-ruta-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesas de Olivenza — circular por el entorno del embalse del lago Alqueva (ruta 2)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 35.1-mile trail that navigates through dehesas near the Alqueva reservoir.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails around Valverde De Leganés?
A: Along the trails, you can explore various points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1341853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Real Sancha Brava&lt;/a&gt; offers historical insights, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3128829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alor Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views. For technical riding, check out the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1614952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Technical Trail Along Embalse de Piedra Aguda&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5427515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olivenza&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable settlement to explore.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the 9 easy trails in the region are generally suitable for families with some cycling experience. These routes tend to be less technical and cover scenic, rural landscapes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails in the Valverde De Leganés area, especially those through dehesas and rural paths, are dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking?
A: The region's dehesas and ecological corridors, such as those along the Alcarrache River, are rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species like black storks, Egyptian vultures, and imperial eagles, as well as mammals such as wild boar, foxes, and rabbits.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain biking options?
A: Yes, for longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027101996/guadiana-nature-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 42 miles (68 km). Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353310684/valverde-de-leganes-olivenza-and-san-jorge-de-alor-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valverde de Leganés, Olivenza, and San Jorge de Alor Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 29.5-mile path connecting several local towns.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Valverde De Leganés?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the serene rural environments, and the engaging variety of terrain, from dehesas to technical single-tracks.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 8 difficult mountain bike trails in the region. These routes often feature more significant elevation gains and technical sections, such as those found along the Piedra Aguda Reservoir or in the Sierra de Alor area.
tips: This small town of less than 5000 inhabitants is surrounded by beautiful fields and landscapes. It is not far from the Piedra Aguda Reservoir and has several interesting sites to see in the city, including the Madre De Dios Franciscan Convent.
Here you will find a nice technical single-track trail that runs alongside the Piedra Aguda Reservoir. Fun, fast and beautiful views.
Here you will find an incredible oak tree. I have no idea how old it is, but it's really worth a visit. Its branches are so spread out that it is necessary to support them.
